name: Segregation of Duties Check
description: After task completion, reminds that code author ≠ reviewer ≠ deployer. Enforces GOV-ROLE segregation rules.

then.prompt:
Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Construction phase onward (Tier 2).

A task was completed. Verify segregation of duties:

1. AUTHOR ≠ REVIEWER: The person who wrote this code MUST NOT review/approve the PR. Check CODEOWNERS for the affected module — is a different person assigned as reviewer?
2. FINANCIAL SEGREGATION: If this task involved financial entities (invoices, payments, journal entries), remind that business logic MUST enforce creator ≠ approver.
3. DEPLOYMENT SEGREGATION: When this work goes to production, the deployer MUST be different from the author.

Report:
- ✅ Segregation rules satisfied by project structure
- ⚠️ Rules that need human confirmation (can't verify automatically)
- ❌ Any violations detected

If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "segregation-check", "trigger": "postTaskExecution", "ruleId": "GOV-ROLE-004", "ruleSeverity": "critical", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}"}
Log ONE event per rule checked.
